pages.raw_text:
E. F. Quinn business agent of the
trades council and president of the
tailors union, said today:


Mayor Byrne should receive the
unhesitating vote of every iaboring
man in this city. No suggestion from
a man who is promised n political
position or who is n member of one
of the old parties should influence
a single labor voter. Never before
has Spokane had a mayor who has
stood for union labor as he has


I'm the present campaign the
chances are too great to change a
certainty for a uncertainty at the
polls tomorrow. and workingmen
should not lose sight of the fact that
the only real issue Is that of the
Commercial club versus organized la
Dor The only way for us to protect
our homes is to see that the homes
are supplied with enough money earn
ed by wages to protect them.


There may be other men who
would follow the policy that Mayor
Byrne has pursued towards organiz-
ed labor but such another mayor
has never been found in Spokane
Union men know this and they also
know if they were to lose Mayor
Byrne as the chief executive of the
city they would be the greatest losers
of all the people in the city. For
that reason every workingman.
whether in the ranks of organized la
Dor or outside and every poor man
should go to the polls tomorrow and
cast his ballot for the mayors re
election.
